titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL L.F.C. Ch. xxx.2

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=618 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:20:20.576335+00 | translation moa=619 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:20:22.130768+00

div.p:
W. dei gratia Rex Scott’ . Episcopis . abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Iusticiis . Ministris . et Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre
sue . Clericis . et laicis . Salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri . me concessisse . et hac carta mea confirmasse deo atque
ecclesie Sancte Marie de fonte clericorum . et Monialibus ibidem de seruientibus . illas . vii . xx . acras terre in Hangre
de totham . quas Robertus filius Sewin de Norhamton’ eis dedit . et de dono eiusdem dimidium Holmum
quem engelramus tenuerat . et . quatuor . truncos ad ignem suum de bosco meo de toteham . et decem porcos
quietos a pasnagio . tenendum in perpetuam elemosinam . ita libere . et quiete . plenarie . et honorifice . sicut Car
ta Regis Malcol’ fratris mei et mea tenuras illas predicto Roberto filio Sewini confirmant . et Carta
eidem Roberti . eis liberius . quiecius . plenius . et honorificencius . testatur . et confirmat  saluo seruicio meo . test’.
Ric’ de Moreu’ constabulario . Ric’ Cuttun . philippo de Valon’. Hugone Ridel . Waltero de berkel’.
Waltero de Windesour’ Roberto de berkel . Willelmo filio Emme . Iohanne de Hasting’. Apud Norhamton’ .

William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, officials, and all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know me to have conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and also to the church of St Mary at Clerkenwell [Clerks'-Well] and the monks serving God there the 140 [⁊ × 20] acres of land in the Hanger of Tottenham that Robert fitz Sewin of Northampton gave them, and of the gift of the same half holm which Ingram had held, and four tree trunks for their fire from my wood at Tottenham, and ten pigs quit of pasture dues, to be held in everlasting alms, as freely and quitly, fully, and honourably, as the charter of King Malcolm my brother and my own confirm those holdings to the aforesaid Robert fitz Sewin, and the charter of the same Robert bears witness and confirms to them freely, quitly, fully, and honourably; saving my service. As witnesses: Richard de Morville the constable; Richard Cotton; Philip de Valognes; Hugh Ridel; Walter of Berkeley; Walter of Windsor; Robert of Berkeley; William son of Emma; John of Hastings. At Northampton.
